description: A vertical, portrait-orientation 'Labor News' headline card from the U.S. Department of Labor. The background is a color photograph of two workers in a manufacturing/industrial setting: a younger woman with curly hair wearing a yellow hard hat, safety glasses, plaid shirt and a high-visibility yellow-green safety vest, standing beside an older bearded white man also in a yellow hard hat, safety glasses and high-vis vest, who appears to be instructing/mentoring her, with industrial control panels behind them. The lower portion has a translucent blue gradient panel. A white rectangle holds 'LABOR NEWS' in blue serif caps; below in bold white serif: 'American Manufacturing Apprenticeship Incentive Fund portal now accepting applications,' then in white italic serif: 'Eligible Registered Apprenticeship sponsors will receive $3,500 for each new apprentice hired,' and finally bold white 'dol.gov/newsroom.' The design mimics a press-release news graphic. The messaging technique is achievement/program-promotion framing the administration as restoring American manufacturing and funding apprenticeships. Target audience is employers, apprenticeship sponsors, workers, and the public.
notable_text: LABOR NEWS; American Manufacturing Apprenticeship Incentive Fund portal now accepting applications; Eligible Registered Apprenticeship sponsors will receive $3,500 for each new apprentice hired; dol.gov/newsroom
